etymology.description: semantic 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") + phonetic 甬(甬) (yǒng, "a place name") -> through; open; connect; communicate
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the movement radical 辶(辵) (chuò, "walk; movement") representing passage or travel with 甬(甬) (yǒng), which provides the sound. Walking or moving through a passage communicates the idea of going through, being open, connecting different places, or allowing communication between them.

examples.chinese: 这条路已经通车了(這條路已經通車了)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù yǐjīng tōngchē le.
examples.english: This road is now open to traffic.

examples.chinese: 我们需要改善沟通效率(我們需要改善溝通效率)。
examples.english: We need to improve communication efficiency.

examples.chinese: 他终于打通了所有的关卡(他終於打通了所有的關卡)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zhōngyú dǎ tōng le suǒyǒu de guānkǎ.
examples.english: He finally got through all the levels.
